Preprocess summary statistics for BRIERs (genotype as predictors)

preprocessS.Rd

Aligns target sumstats and external model coefficients to a target LD reference panel for use with BRIERs. The LD reference panel defines the canonical allele orientation; target and external inputs are matched by chromosome and position, then sign-flipped where their alleles are swapped relative to the LD reference.

Usage

preprocessS(
  target.ss,
  target.ind = c("gwas", "corr"),
  target.ld,
  external.ss = NULL,
  target.ss.cols = c(chr = "CHR", bp = "BP", ref = "REF", alt = "ALT", p = "pval", n =
    "n", sgn = "sgn", beta = "beta", corr = "corr"),
  target.ld.cols = c(chr = "CHR", bp = "BP", ref = "REF", alt = "ALT"),
  external.ss.cols = c(chr = "CHR", bp = "BP", ref = "REF", alt = "ALT"),
  external.coef.cols = NULL,
  drop.ambiguous = TRUE,
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

target.ss

A data.frame of target summary statistics. Must contain chromosome, basepair, REF, ALT, plus value columns determined by target.ind.

target.ind

One of "gwas" or "corr". For "gwas", target.ss must contain p-value, sample size, and either sign or beta columns. For "corr", a correlation column is required.

target.ld

A data.frame of the target LD reference panel SNP info, defining the canonical allele orientation. Must contain chromosome, basepair, REF, ALT.

external.ss

Optional data.frame of external model coefficients. Must contain chromosome, basepair, REF, ALT, plus one or more coefficient columns.

target.ss.cols

Named character vector mapping canonical keys (chr, bp, ref, alt, p, n, sgn, beta, corr) to the column names in target.ss. Only the keys relevant to target.ind need to be present in the data.

target.ld.cols

Named character vector mapping chr, bp, ref, alt to column names in target.ld.

external.ss.cols

Named character vector mapping chr, bp, ref, alt to column names in external.ss.

external.coef.cols

Character vector of column names in external.ss that hold model coefficients. Required when external.ss is supplied; the function does not auto-detect.

drop.ambiguous

Logical. If TRUE (default), drops strand-ambiguous SNPs (A/T, T/A, C/G, G/C alleles) from every input before matching.

verbose

Logical. If TRUE (default), prints SNP counts at each filter step.

Value

A list with the following elements:

target.ss

Processed target sumstats as a data.frame with columns varnames, CHR, BP, REF, ALT, corr (in the LD reference orientation).

target.ld

Processed target LD reference panel SNP info, subset to the surviving SNPs, with columns varnames, CHR, BP, REF, ALT.

target.ld.keep

Integer vector of indices into the original target.ld for the surviving SNPs. Use as LD[target.ld.keep, target.ld.keep] to subset a pre-computed LD matrix.

external.ss

Processed external coefficients (or NULL if no external input was supplied, or if every supplied external model had all-zero coefficients after alignment), with columns varnames, CHR, BP, REF, ALT, coef1, coef2, ... where surviving coefficient columns are renumbered coef1, ..., coefK in input order.

external.coef.names

Named character vector mapping the standardized column names (coef1, coef2, ...) back to the original column names supplied in external.coef.cols, or NULL if no external input survived.

summary

Named integer vector of SNP counts: n.input.tss, n.input.tld, n.input.ess, n.dropped.invalid.chr, n.dropped.ambiguous, n.dropped.multiallelic, n.dropped.unmatched.tss, n.aligned, n.flipped, n.final, n.external.matched, n.external.flipped, n.external.conflict, n.external.zero.

Details

When target.ind = "gwas", a correlation column (corr) is computed from the p-value, sample size, and effect direction via p2cor; the rest of the pipeline then treats GWAS and correlation inputs uniformly. P-values of exactly zero are imputed with the smallest observed non-zero p-value. If every p-value is zero, the function stops with an error.

Inputs are validated for missing values: if NA is detected in any of the required columns of target.ss, target.ld, or external.ss, the function stops with an error. Clean the inputs before calling.

Chromosome coding is normalized internally: "chr" prefix is stripped, PLINK numeric codes (23/24/26) mapped to "X"/"Y"/"MT", and "M" normalized to "MT". SNPs with unrecognized CHR values (anything not in "1"\(-\)"22", "X", "Y", "MT", "XY") are dropped with a warning. varnames in the output uses the canonical form.

Three categories of input are handled:

Target sumstats (target.ss)

Kept only at SNPs present in both the target sumstats and the LD reference. SNPs absent from either side are dropped (no zero-imputation). Surviving rows have their corr sign-flipped where the target's REF/ALT is swapped relative to the LD reference.

Target LD reference (target.ld)

Defines the canonical allele orientation. The returned LD panel is a subset of the input containing only the SNPs that survived alignment with the target sumstats. The original-row indices are returned in target.ld.keep so callers can subset a pre-computed LD matrix.

External coefficients (external.ss)

Aligned to the surviving SNP set. Coefficient columns are sign-flipped where alleles are swapped, and zero-padded for SNPs that the external model does not cover. Output coefficient columns are renamed to coef1, coef2, ... in input order; the mapping back to the original column names is returned in external.coef.names.

External models whose coefficient column is entirely zero after alignment (no overlap with the surviving target SNP set) are dropped from the output. If all supplied external models are dropped, the function emits a warning and returns external.ss = NULL.

All three output data.frames share a varnames column of the form "CHR:BP:REF:ALT" (in the LD reference orientation) as the first column, providing a stable row identifier across the outputs.

Multi-allelic variants (duplicate CHR:BP entries) are dropped with a warning from every input independently. Strand-ambiguous SNPs (alleles A/T or C/G) are dropped by default; set drop.ambiguous = FALSE to keep them when strand is reliably matched across studies.

See also

BRIERs, calLD, p2cor, preprocessI, mergeExternals

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
out <- preprocessS(
  target.ss          = my_gwas,
  target.ind         = "gwas",
  target.ld          = hapmap3_panel,
  external.ss        = prs_coefs,
  external.coef.cols = c("PRS_EUR", "PRS_EAS"),
  target.ss.cols     = c(chr = "Chromosome", bp = "Position", p = "PVAL")
)

XtY      <- out$target.ss$corr
XtX      <- precomputed_LD[out$target.ld.keep, out$target.ld.keep]
beta.ext <- as.matrix(
  out$external.ss[, grep("^coef", colnames(out$external.ss))]
)

fit <- BRIERs(XtX = XtX, XtY = XtY, beta.external = beta.ext, ...)
} # }
